bullets.text: Strait of Hormuz traffic halts; oil-tanker charter rates surge, signaling higher shipping/energy risk premia and potential inflation spillovers.
Pro-U.S. Kurdish forces prepare a possible incursion into Iran, raising escalation risk and widening regional conflict scenarios.
Iran’s state media and social platforms push a defiant, distorted war narrative, highlighting information-operations intensity and domestic-control tactics.
China sets sub-5% growth target amid deflation and tariff headwinds, implying weaker global demand impulse and more pressure for domestic stimulus.
China to boost defense spending ~7% (slowest since 2021), signaling sustained military priorities even as growth targets and fiscal space tighten.
Global 15% tariff begins this week; Treasury signals possible rollback later, injecting near-term trade-cost inflation risk and policy uncertainty for firms.
U.S. court takes steps toward ordering tariff refunds, potentially creating sizable fiscal liabilities and altering corporate expectations about trade-policy durability.
Trump nominates Kevin Warsh for Fed chair, raising uncertainty about future monetary-policy reaction functions and market pricing of rate paths.
White House A.I. industry pledge aims to fund power needs; grid constraints and permitting become central bottlenecks for data-center growth.
Broadcom beats earnings; A.I. revenue doubles and custom chips stay hot, supporting continued capex cycle for accelerators and networking.
Blue Owl private-credit stumble revives systemic-risk fears, spotlighting liquidity/maturity-mismatch vulnerabilities in fast-growing nonbank credit markets.
NRC grants first commercial reactor construction approval in 10 years; TerraPower Wyoming project clears a key permitting hurdle for advanced nuclear deployment.
Study suggests many patients maintain weight loss with fewer GLP-1 injections, potentially lowering treatment costs and improving long-term adherence.
Climate models may undercount carbon from boreal wildfires; if confirmed, remaining carbon budgets shrink and climate-risk pricing could intensify.
